# UC-12.4.15 · Policy-as-Code Violation Trending

## Description

OPA, Sentinel, or Conftest denials over time—spikes after new policy rollout (extends UC-12.4.5).

## Value

OPA, Sentinel, or Conftest denials over time—spikes after new policy rollout (extends UC-12.4.5).

## Implementation

Baseline failures per policy. Alert on 3× week-over-week spike. Run education before switching to hard-fail.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OPA decision logs, Terraform Cloud policy sets.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `result="fail"`, `policy_path`, `namespace`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Baseline failures per policy. Alert on 3× week-over-week spike. Run education before switching to hard-fail.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="opa:decision"
| where result="fail"
| timechart span=1d count by policy_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Policy-as-Code Violation Trending** — OPA, Sentinel, or Conftest denials over time—spikes after new policy rollout (extends UC-12.4.5).

Documented **Data sources**: `result="fail"`, `policy_path`, `namespace`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OPA decision logs, Terraform Cloud policy sets. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iac; **sourcetype**: opa:decision. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iac, sourcetype="opa:decision". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where result="fail"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by policy_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with Terraform Cloud audit and run history. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (violations over time), Bar chart (by policy), Table (top namespaces).

## SPL

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="opa:decision"
| where result="fail"
| timechart span=1d count by policy_name
```

## Visualization

Line chart (violations over time), Bar chart (by policy), Table (top namespaces).
